concept: Hammer / Striking Tool / To Strike for Shaping
description: A tool with a heavy head for striking; to strike with force for shaping. 'Toka' names what shapes through impact—controlled force building and forming.
sound_symbolism: The word strikes defined. 'To' lands with clear impact, hammer falling. Then 'ka' marks the result, shape emerging from strike.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'toka' (the hammer). As verb: 'mia keluo toka.' (I hammer the metal.) Used for building, forging, constructive shaping.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Building community—hammer as tool of construction, creating shelter and infrastructure.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The hammer builds—from blacksmith to carpenter, shaping material through patient striking.
